description: §51.0.H edge-case (iii) / §51.0.J — a boot `effect=` on a DERIVED-engine opener is E-ENGINE-EFFECT-ON-DERIVED. A `derived=` engine has no init->initial= edge (its value is COMPUTED, not entered) and forbids writes to its variable, so a boot effect has nothing coherent to do. This is the derived-engine counterpart to the plain-engine boot effect that `boot-effect` exercises positively. (W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED co-fires incidentally from the sibling `<program>`; @count is read+written so no dead-cell E-DG-002.)
rationale: §51.0.H (iii): `effect=` on a `derived=` engine opener is E-ENGINE-EFFECT-ON-DERIVED. A derived engine has no init->initial= edge and forbids writes to its variable (E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-WRITE), so a boot effect is incoherent. Use a plain mount-time `${}` effect at the enclosing scope, or a non-derived engine, instead.
